D10 Imaging L4 Analyze Reference

Bayer Demosaicing

a camera sensor sees only one colour per pixel — the rest is guessed, and the guess shows on every diagonal edge

A colour image sensor is a grid of monochrome photosites under a colour filter array. The classic Bayer pattern repeats RGGB every 2×2 — half the sites measure green (close to luminance), a quarter measure red, a quarter blue. Demosaicing interpolates the missing two channels at every pixel from its neighbours; smooth areas look fine, edges trip the algorithm.

Compare nearest-neighbour to bilinear demosaicing on the same raw mosaic. The diagonal edge in the test pattern produces colour fringes ("zipper") under bilinear and blocky colour blobs under nearest — the canonical demosaic failure modes.
